reasoning.typography: Strong serif headlines with deliberate weight and distinctive underline treatment on 'research' and 'products'. More character than Inter+Fraunces, though body copy feels safe.
reasoning.hierarchy: Hero headline dominates effectively with scale and weight. Mission statement provides good counterbalance. Card hierarchy in 'Latest releases' is clear but predictable.
reasoning.distinctiveness: The underlined 'research and products' treatment and 'Project Glasswing' naming show personality, but the overall structure (hero → project showcase → card grid) could work for many tech brands. Mission statement language is specific though.
reasoning.color: Restrained beige/cream base with black accents. Black hero section provides good contrast. Minimal color palette shows discipline, though borders on corporate-safe.
reasoning.density: Generous breathing room in hero, confident use of negative space in black section. Card grid has good padding. Avoids the overcrowded trap, though maybe too uniform in lower sections.
reasoning.voice: Structure is fairly conventional (hero/feature/releases grid). The 'public benefit corporation' framing and safety-first messaging provide voice, but IA still follows tech-company template. No 'Currently/Selected/Testimonials' but close.

winning_patterns: Underline typographic treatment creates instant visual identity without gimmicks
Black section with subtle geometric visualization breaks up beige monotony effectively
Mission statement positioning (right-aligned, secondary hierarchy) shows restraint
Dates on cards provide temporal context instead of generic 'Learn more' spam
anti_patterns: Three-column card grid is the default Webflow/Framer layout — zero invention
Navigation structure is generic tech company (Research/News/Try Product)
'Latest releases' as a section title is pure CMS thinking, not storytelling
Card design (beige backgrounds, safe padding) could be any SaaS marketing site
Project name 'Glasswing' floats without context or explanation — assumes insider knowledge
